predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: There's going to be one juror who pulls up blame on him [Trump].
predictions.context: Bob is discussing the Trump hush money trial, arguing that the jury cannot be objective. He predicts at least one juror will hold out against conviction.
predictions.timeframe: by the end of the trial

predictions.speaker: Chad Kultgen
predictions.prediction: I think it's probably going to be a hung jury mistrial scenario.
predictions.context: Chad is discussing the Trump trial and his assessment of the jury's likely outcome, based on his belief that an objective jury is impossible.
predictions.timeframe: by the end of the trial

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: He [Judge Merchan] is not going to put Trump in jail. He doesn't have the balls to do it.
predictions.context: Bob is reacting to the judge's warning that Trump could be jailed for further gag order violations. He asserts the judge lacks the courage to follow through.

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: That [jailing Trump] would guarantee us a win.
predictions.context: Bob claims that if Judge Merchan jails Trump for contempt, it would ensure a political victory for Trump's supporters.

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: I think five games into the season, that'll wear off.
predictions.context: Bob is discussing Caitlin Clark's impact on WNBA attendance, predicting the initial surge in fan interest will fade quickly.

predictions.speaker: Chad Kultgen
predictions.prediction: I think she's [Caitlin Clark] going to invigorate the WNBA with a new fan base.
predictions.context: Chad disagrees with his father, predicting Caitlin Clark will sustainably increase interest and viewership for the WNBA.

predictions.speaker: Bob Kultgen
predictions.prediction: They're going to blow us up somewhere. They really will.
predictions.context: Bob asserts that terrorists are entering the U.S. across the southern border and are waiting to execute a major attack.

predictions.speaker: Mary Lou Kultgen
predictions.prediction: I truly believe a lot of people are on anti-depression medicine and that medicine makes you do weird stuff.
predictions.context: Mary Lou is speculating on causes for violent crime, predicting a link between antidepressant medication and violent behavior.
